title: referrals — retention windows
description: Referrals data is financial-audit-adjacent. Every issued reward is a compliance-relevant financial event (tax audit, ad-spend audit, fraud audit). Default retention 7 years (SOC 2 CC6.3 + national tax law baselines: US, EU, UK, CA). Enterprise tier extends to 10y via `referrals_extended_retention`. TenantErased cascades FK CASCADE hard-delete of every owned row EXCEPT paid rewards — those migrate to a compliance archive for legal-obligation retention.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) — storage limitation (retention windows justified by tax + fraud audit)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention carve-out)
gdpr Art. 6(1)(b) — lawful basis: contract (reward program = contractual relationship)
can-spam Act — invitation emails carry unsubscribe path
us tax law — 7-year financial-record retention (1099 reporting for rewards > $600)
iso 27001 Annex A.12.3 — audit log retention
ccpa 1798.100 — right to know + right to delete (with retention carve-out)

purge.trigger: > 7 (or 10) years past archived_at + zero downstream references
purge.notes: Every issued reward traces back to a program. Program cannot be hard-deleted while any reward retains a reference — retention aligns with the longest-lived downstream (rewards + fraud flags).
Codes retain for audit — a paid reward may reference the code as its acquisition path.

purge.trigger: > 7 (or 10) years past claimed_at + status IN (rewarded / cancelled / expired / fraudulent)
purge.notes: Retention runs against claimed_at — the earliest financial-relevant timestamp. Fraudulent referrals also retain 7y (fraud audit trail alignment).

hot.description: Active + first year for dispute resolution

purge.action: hard delete OR migrate to compliance_archive.referral_rewards (paid rewards)
purge.notes: Paid rewards over $600 (US tax threshold) migrate to compliance_archive rather than delete — 1099 reporting requires the ledger to persist. Cancelled + clawback rewards may be hard-deleted at the retention boundary.

hot.days: while parent Referral in hot window

purge.notes: Fraud audit trail. Retained per SOC 2 CC6.3 + ISO 27001 Annex A.12.3 (audit log retention). Reviewed flags with disposition=confirmed_fraud retain longer — the Enterprise `referrals_extended_retention` extends to 10y.

purge.action: cascaded (JSONB column on the referral row)
purge.notes: The snapshot is IMMUTABLE — no in-flight edits ever. Under GDPR right-to-erasure, the RedactReferralDataOnUserErasure hook redacts referred_email to '[REDACTED]' at 90 days post-erasure but retains the utm/click_id fields for aggregate rollup analytics.

device_snapshot stores only HASHES (SHA-256 of UA + IP + fingerprint), never raw values. Retention aligns with the referral row + fraud flag rows. Under user-erasure: hashes stay (they can't be re-derived), but the referred_email field zeros out.

special_paths.action: FK CASCADE hard-deletes every referral_programs / referral_codes / referrals / referral_rewards / referral_fraud_flags row.
special_paths.carve_out: PAID rewards (finance_credit_id set + paid_at set) migrate to compliance_archive.referral_rewards prior to cascade. Audit trail rows survive per audit module design (outlives-source).

special_paths.action: RedactReferralDataOnUserErasure hook: cancel pending referrals + retain paid rewards with subject redacted (recipient_id nulled but role preserved).
special_paths.carve_out: Retention windows on the referral / reward rows unchanged — financial retention overrides erasure per legal-obligation carve-out.

special_paths.action: Redact referred_email + referred_id on Referral row 90 days post-erasure. Keep the fraud flags for fraud audit trail.
Retention windows do NOT reset — the archive is a business-lifecycle event, not a retention-lifecycle event. The 7 (or 10) year window continues to run from claimed_at on each referral.

cross_reference: audit::retention.json — audit rows outlive their source rows regardless (fraud audit + financial audit).
activity::retention.json — activity rows follow their own tier-based retention.
compliance::consent-registry retention — consent snapshots on referrals retain per referral retention.
growth::attribution retention — attribution touchpoints retain 730d default / 7y with extended-retention entitlement.
